titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Emir Rodriguez Monegal Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Matthew T. McLean

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:35

descriptivenote.p: The collection is in Spanish and Portuguese.

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence and subject files related to the professional life of Emir Rodriguez Monegal as an expert in Latin American literature and language.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was transferred from Bryn Mawr, 1996.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research with the exception of the correspondence series, which is restricted until January 1, 2059.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Emir Rodriguez Monegal Papers (MS 1750). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in four series: I. Correspondence, 1959-1983. II. Organizations, 1964-1985. III. Yale University, 1964-1985. IV. Subject Files, 1968-1985.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Emir Rodriguez Monegal was born in Melo, Uruguay, in 1921. He was a member of the Yale University faculty from 1969 until his death in 1985. At various times Rodriguez Monegal served as chairman and director of graduate studies of the Department of Spanish and Portuguese. He was a visiting professor at several universities, including Harvard University, the University of Southern California, El Colegio de Mexico, Universidade Federal in Rio de Janeiro, and the University of Pittsburgh. Rodriguez Monegal was an expert on Latin American literature and the author of a literary biography of Jorge Luis Borges and several other books. He also served as editor of numerous literary magazines. He married Selma Calasans and had three children. Rodriguez Monegal died in New Haven on November 21, 1985.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence and subject files related to the professional life of Emir Rodriguez Monegal as an expert in Latin American literature and language.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains correspondence related to the professional life of Emir Rodriguez Monegal and is arranged in alphabetical order by last name of correspondent.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted until January 1, 2059.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains correspondence and other materials related to the professional organizations with which Rodriguez Monegal was affiliated, and is arranged in alphabetical order by name of organization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains materials related to faculty committees with which Rodriguez Monegal worked and other aspects of his professional life at Yale University, and is arranged in alphabetical order by subject.

did.unittitle: Concilium on International and Area Studies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains materials related to the professional life of Emir Rodriguez Monegal, and is arranged in alphabetical order by subject.
